executive_summary: Your amber status is driven entirely by supplier concentration risk, not macro conditions or peer instability. Three clusters demand immediate attention: (1) China-exposed EMS and battery suppliers (Huizhou BYD, Smoore, EVE Energy) face critical battery exposure under active US-China trade pressure and Taiwan risk—this is your highest-priority vulnerability given how embedded these are in your supply chain; (2) printing substrate suppliers across three geographies (Stora Enso in Finland showing a hard 8.2% stock crash with no confirmed cause, plus IP Sun and Sappi under geopolitical strain) suggest either a sector-wide shock or localized disruption you need to diagnose within 48 hours; (3) nicotine and filter materials from India, South Korea, and China are secondary but flagged by border and regional military tensions. The unconfirmed stock crashes at Stora Enso and Infineon are more urgent than abstract geopolitical flags—escalate calls to those suppliers immediately to rule out operational breakdown or hidden capacity loss, then assess whether you need alternate sources for printing substrates or can absorb a brief shortage.
